# Net-handler depth audit — results

*Synthesis of three per-opcode net-handler audits (entities · world-map · session-ui), extending the Milestone-1 "protocol primitives proven" result up to the packet handlers. Ground truth = the jar's clean Vineflower decompile (`decompiled/normalized-clean/client.java`, obf `client.b(int,byte,int)` = `handlePacket`, `client.a(int,int,int)` = `onFriendUpdate`). Subject = `src/client/Mudclient.java`. Oracles = OpenRSC `Payload235Generator` / `GameStateUpdater` and LeadingBot `mudclient.java` (rev ~235) / mudclient204.*

---

## 1. Coverage and tallies

The two incoming dispatchers (`handlePacket` master + `onFriendUpdate` social/login pre-dispatcher) and their entity helper methods were diffed read-for-read against the clean decompile, with every obf buffer-accessor letter mapped to an exact width via `net/Buffer.java` / `net/BitBuffer.java`. Three groups, de-duplicated:

| Group | Surface | Audited units | MATCH | MINOR | DIVERGENCE |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **entities** | player/NPC sync + helpers + entity-adjacent decode | 19 verdict units (5 core opcodes 191/234/104/79/83; 4 helpers getPlayer/addPlayer/addNpc/getNpc; 8 adjacent 53/156/153/25/59/99/91/211; 234 sub-types 0–7) | 19 | 0 | 1 in-group (+1 cross-listed: 48) |
| **world-map** | region/scene state | 7 (191, loadRegion, 48, 91, 99, 211, 234 proj sub-types 3&4) | 4 | 2 | 3 |
| **session-ui** | login/stats/inv/bank/shop/trade/duel/dialogue/chat/friends/settings/sleep/logout | 55 opcodes (44 master + 11 social) + 1149 | 52 | 1 | 2 |

**Consolidated, de-duplicated totals** (opcodes counted once even when probed by two groups; 234's sub-types resolved as a unit):

- **Total distinct incoming opcodes / handler units audited: ~70** — 191, 234 (8 sub-types), 104, 79, 83, 48, 91, 99, 211, loadRegion, 4 entity helpers, plus the 55 session-ui opcodes (111/53/245/252/25/156/153/59/92/128/97/162/101/137/15/240/206/5/42/203/33/176/225/20/6/30/249/90/123/159/253/210/172/204/89/222/114/117/244/84/194/52/213 and social 131/4/183/189/165/149/237/109/120/51/87).
- **MATCH: ~62** (incl. all 4 crypto-adjacent helpers, the full 191/79/104/83/211 entity stream, 234 sub-types {0,1,2,3,4,5,7}, projectile sub-types 3&4, and 52/55 session-ui opcodes).
- **MINOR (cosmetic / behaviourally-equivalent): 4** — loadRegion rebase-loop comment mislabels; opcode-91 dir stored `int` vs jar `byte` (sign-extension identical for real dir values); opcode-149 `var1^87`→`0` constant fold (exact under the dispatcher's `a==87` caller invariant); plus the pervasive (non-counting) trade/duel/shop *comment* label swaps vs OpenRSC's `OpcodeOut` names — code writes the same fields the jar writes regardless of label.
- **DIVERGENCE (semantic): 5** — listed and ranked in §2.

**One naming truth that recurs and is NOT a divergence:** the jar binds **99 = boundary/wall** and **91 = ground-item**, the reverse of OpenRSC's `OpcodeOut` enum labels (which call 91 BOUNDARY / 99 GROUND_ITEM). The deob faithfully follows the **jar** (ground truth); only inline doc comments carry the OpenRSC labels. Verified consistent across both the entities and world-map audits.

---

## 2. Consolidated severity-ranked DIVERGENCE list

Ranked by world-state blast radius. **[WP]** = world-perception divergence — flag for render/cradle work, because it corrupts what the client believes exists in the scene, which the renderer then draws (or fails to draw).

### CRITICAL

**D1 — Opcode 48 SEND_SCENERY_HANDLER: entire handler MISSING. [WP]**
- Surface: `Mudclient.java` `handlePacket` has no opcode-48 branch — the dispatch jumps from 99 (`return` @4247) straight to 111 (@4250). The jar's handler is `client.java:14510-14659` (encoded `if(-49==~var1)`, which is why a plain `==48` grep misses it). Oracle: LeadingBot `mudclient.java:1537-1607` (`!=60000` add gate).
- Why it matters: this is the entire server-driven scenery add/remove stream — trees, signs, furniture, fences, door-*objects*, with their per-model orientation (`Hh.b(x,y)`), dimensions (`ub.g[]`/`f.f[]`), the type-74 special case, and lighting written into `Se/ye/vc/bg/hg[eh++]`. With it absent, `eh`/`Se`/`ye`/`vc`/`hg[]` are only ever read, reset, or compacted — never appended from the live region stream. **Scenery never appears or updates mid-session, and the 99/211 handlers that re-cull `eh` operate on permanently-stale scenery.** This is the single largest world-perception hole found. (Cross-listed by the entities auditor as out-of-its-scope but inside the audited dispatch; owned by world-map.)

### HIGH

**D2 — Opcode 91 ground-item add gate WRONG. [WP]**
- Surface: `Mudclient.java:4413/4427/4433` vs jar `client.java:15009-15022`. Oracle LeadingBot `mudclient.java:1967`.
- The deob adds the item `if(!placed)` (where `placed` = a match was found at the tile); the jar adds `if(itemId != 65535)`. Two concrete failure modes: (a) a re-sent item on an occupied tile (real id, match found) → jar removes-then-re-adds (item persists, model rebuilt), deob removes and **skips re-add → item disappears**; (b) a pure-remove sentinel `65535` on an empty tile → jar adds nothing, deob `placed==false` → **spawns a phantom item id 65535** (bogus model / likely `buildEntityModel` fault). Read order/widths are correct; only the gate predicate is wrong.

**D3 — Opcode 99 boundary removal-run 1-byte buffer desync. [WP]**
- Surface: spurious `this.mg.w--` at `Mudclient.java:4188` vs jar `client.java:14385` + `14471-14473` (no un-read); mudclient204 confirms `l+=3`.
- In the marker==255 removal-run, the deob un-reads the 0xFF marker before the two anchor `h()` reads, so `h()` reads the marker byte itself as anchorX (→ `Lf + (-1)>>3`, wrong tile culled), reads the real anchorX as anchorY, and **leaves the real anchorY byte unconsumed → the boundary stream desyncs by 1 byte for the remainder of the packet**, cascading corruption through every subsequent wall/door add/remove in that packet. Internally inconsistent: the deob's own opcode-91 removal-run (`:4382`) correctly has *no* `w--` — strong evidence this is a transcription bug, not a deliberate variant.

### MEDIUM

**D4 — Opcode 137 SEND_SHOP_CLOSE: wrong handler body (phantom byte + wrong field). Session-UI.**
- Surface: `Mudclient.java:4742-4746` vs jar `client.java:15661-15663` (`~var1==-138`).
- The jar reads **zero** payload bytes and clears the shop/search-list-open flag `uk` (`uk=false; return`). The deob instead reads one byte (`h(20869)`=`readRawByte`) and sets the trade/duel `Mi` flag — its body is a verbatim copy of opcode 15. SEND_SHOP_CLOSE has an empty payload, so the extra read is a **1-byte over-read** (off-by-one into the next packet on a multi-packet buffer — the only divergence in this audit with a buffer-width consequence on session-ui), and the shop/list never closes (`uk` stays set; `uk` gates the list render path, jar `1372` / deob `7026`). Not world-perception, but it does shift the byte cursor.

### LOW–MEDIUM

**D5 — Opcode 234 sub-type 6 (self-speech) AND opcode 101 (`uk` lifecycle). Session-UI / chat.**
Two distinct low-trigger semantic writes, grouped:
- **234 sub-type 6** (`Mudclient.java:4334` vs jar `client.java:14833-14837`): the deob reads `ia.a(mg,false)` (a variable-length scrambled string) **before** the `player != null` check; the jar reads nothing when the cache slot is null (`if (type!=6 || player==null) break`). A type-6 update for a null/out-of-view `We[idx]` slot consumes a string the jar leaves in place, **desyncing every remaining entry in that 234 packet**. Narrow trigger (type 6 is the local player's own chat echo, whose slot is normally populated) but a genuine byte-faithfulness break. Note: all other 234 sub-types (0–5, 7) and opcode-104 type-1 correctly mirror the jar's null-skip — the divergence is isolated to sub-type 6.
- **Opcode 101** (`Mudclient.java:4715` vs jar `client.java:15534`): an extra `uk=false` after the handler's own `uk=true`, so after a list-open packet the deob leaves `uk==false` where the jar leaves `uk==true`. No buffer-width change. Combined with D4, the whole `uk` open(101)/close(137) UI state lifecycle is inverted — consistent with the author having relocated 137's `uk=false` into 101 and overwritten 137 with opcode-15's body.

---

## 3. Updated confidence statement for the net surface

**Milestone-1 proved the floor.** M1 measured the Tier-0 net **primitives** byte-identical: 48/48 crypto/protocol constants byte-faithful to the jar; ISAAC/Buffer/BitBuffer/XTEA/RSA byte-identical to two oracles (mudclient204, OpenRSC rev-235) **and** to the jar's own obf classes (`o`/`tb`/`ja`) by reflection; and the net decompile-diff = **empty semantic delta** at the primitive level. M1 explicitly named its own gap: *"equivalence proven at the primitive level; full opcode/packet-handler parity (the ~100 message IDs in `client`) is not yet diffed,"* and named the closing gate as a *"per-opcode handler decompile-diff of the `client` message dispatch vs jar."* **This audit is that gate.**

**Does the handler audit extend the empty net delta up to the full protocol? Partially — and honestly, no, not to "empty."** It converts the handler surface from *estimate* to *measured*, and the result is **strong but not clean**: ~62 of ~70 handler units are byte-faithful MATCH (every entity-movement stream 191/79/104/83/211, all four entity helpers, the full stat/inventory/equipment/world-info path, and 52/55 session-ui opcodes). But unlike the primitive layer, the handler layer is **not** an empty semantic delta — it carries **5 real divergences**, including one **missing handler (opcode 48)** and **two 1-byte buffer desyncs (99 removal-run, 137 empty-payload over-read)**. So the honest verdict is:

> **The net surface is proven byte-identical at the primitive layer (M1) and proven *substantially* faithful at the handler layer (this audit) — but the handler layer is NOT yet empty-delta. Five semantic gaps remain, concentrated in world/scene-state opcodes (48/91/99) and shop-UI state (137/101), plus one narrow chat-path read-order break (234.6).**

Decode-direction note: this audit covered the **incoming** (server→client) dispatch in full. The **outgoing** path is partially evidenced — `handleSceneUpdates` (`b(boolean,int)`) was confirmed to be the outgoing right-click menu-action sender (249/53/247/180/161/14/127/99/115/136…) and reads no incoming stream — but the outgoing opcode bodies were **not** field-diffed in these three groups. That is a named residual gap (§5).

Bottom line for confidence: **upgrade the net/protocol surface from M1's "HIGH — proven (primitives)" to "HIGH — proven primitives + measured-faithful handlers with 5 catalogued, fixable divergences."** It is no longer an estimate. It is not yet clean.

---

## 4. Recommended fixes

All five are localized transcription/logic fixes against an identified jar line — none implicate the deobfuscation method or the primitive layer.

- **D1 / opcode 48 (CRITICAL, [WP]):** Transcribe the missing handler from jar `client.java:14510-14659` into `handlePacket` between the 99 and 111 branches. Port the full add path (model build via `kh[fb.f[type]].b(-2)`, dims from `ub.g[]`/`f.f[]`, orientation `Hh.b(x,y)`, the type-74 special case, the `objType!=60000` add gate) and the marker==255 cull path, writing `Se/ye/vc/bg/hg[eh++]`. Highest priority — it is the largest world-perception hole and unblocks meaningful scene rendering.
- **D2 / opcode 91 (HIGH, [WP]):** Replace the `if(!placed)` add gate with `if(itemId != 65535)` at `Mudclient.java:4433` (matching jar `client.java:15009-15022`), preserving the remove-then-re-add semantics for occupied tiles.
- **D3 / opcode 99 (HIGH, [WP]):** Delete the spurious `this.mg.w--` at `Mudclient.java:4188`. The marker must be consumed (not un-read) before the two anchor `h()` reads — mirror the deob's own correct 91 removal-run at `:4382`.
- **D4 / opcode 137 (MEDIUM):** Replace the body at `Mudclient.java:4742-4746` with `this.uk = false; return;` (no byte read), per jar `client.java:15661-15663`. This both removes the empty-payload over-read and restores shop/list close.
- **D5 / opcode 234.6 + opcode 101 (LOW–MEDIUM):** (a) Move the `ia.a(this.mg,false)` read inside the `player != null` guard at `Mudclient.java:4334` (mirror jar `client.java:14833`: skip the read entirely when null). (b) Delete the extra `uk=false` at `Mudclient.java:4715` so opcode 101 leaves `uk==true`. Fix D4 and D5(b) together — they are the two halves of the broken `uk` open/close lifecycle.

Suggested order: D1 → D3 → D2 → D4+D5(b) → D5(a). D1/D2/D3 are the world-perception fixes the render/cradle work depends on; do them before any framebuffer diff so the renderer is fed a correct scene.

---

## 5. What still needs end-to-end live/replay traffic to fully close

Static decompile-diff is sufficient to **find** these divergences but cannot, by itself, close the surface to "empty delta." Remaining items:

1. **Replay/live byte-cursor validation after the fixes.** D3, D4, and D5(a) are *cursor-desync* bugs — their real-world severity depends on packet framing and whether the desyncing branch fires within a multi-entry/multi-packet read. A static diff proves the read order is wrong; only a **replay traffic diff** (drive the fixed deob `handlePacket` and the jar `client.b(...)` over an identical captured server stream and assert identical post-packet buffer offsets + identical world-state mutations) proves the cursor stays aligned end-to-end. M1 confirmed `client.<clinit>` loads cleanly on JDK 17, so the jar side is available by reflection for exactly this harness.
2. **Trigger the narrow branches.** 234.6 (null `We[idx]` on self-speech) and 91's two failure modes (re-send on occupied tile; 65535 on empty tile) are low-probability in normal play; a replay corpus must be *chosen or fuzzed* to exercise them, or they will pass a happy-path live test while still being wrong.
3. **The outgoing opcode bodies.** This audit field-diffed the **incoming** dispatch only. The outgoing senders (`handleSceneUpdates` action opcodes and the login/auth send path that consumes the M1-proven RSA/ISAAC primitives) need a parallel per-opcode write-side diff before the *full bidirectional* protocol can be called proven.
4. **Compile gate (prerequisite, from M1).** M1's "0/8 packages compile, ~7 genuine drift sites" blocker still stands. The replay harness in (1) requires the deob to compile; resolving the subpackage-visibility split remains the cheapest unblock and is a hard prerequisite for any end-to-end diff.

**Net:** the handler audit moves the protocol surface from "primitives proven, handlers estimated" to "primitives proven, handlers measured-faithful with 5 catalogued fixes." Applying the five fixes plus a post-fix replay byte-cursor diff (and the outgoing-side diff) is what would finally extend M1's empty-semantic-delta result from the primitives all the way up through the full incoming protocol.
